s1.ptxt: Fix armature plate (2) with special tool and remove armature plate nut (1).
Remove circlip (2) using snap ring pliers.
Remove magnet clutch pulley (1) using puller.
Remove circlip (2) using snap ring pliers.
Remove magnet clutch lead wire clamp (1).
Remove magnet clutch coil (3) from A/C compressor.
Install magnet clutch coil (2) by fitting protrusion (3) of magnet clutch coil onto hole (4) in A/C compressor.
Install new circlip (1), directing the plane side upward using snap ring pliers.
Press-fit magnet clutch pulley (1) using special tool and hydraulic press.
Install new circlip (1), directing the plane side downward using snap ring pliers.
Install same amount of removing shims.
Tighten new armature plate nut (1) to specified torque.
Adjust clearance between armature plate (1) and magnet clutch pulley (2) by putting shim(s) on compressor shaft. To measure the clearance, perform the following steps.

s2.ptxt: Put A/C compressor in a soft jawed vise (3).
Set dial gauge (4) on armature plate, and then adjust its pointer at 0.
Connect battery positive terminal (+) to magnet clutch coil lead wire.
Connect battery negative terminal (–) from compressor body assembly. (At this point, armature plate and magnet clutch pulley are kept in contact.)
Disconnect battery negative terminal (–) to compressor body assembly. (At this point, armature plate and magnet clutch pulley are not in contact.)
Read stroke of armature plate from dial gauge by performing step d) and e) repeatedly. (Stroke of armature plate indicates clearance between armature plate and magnet clutch pulley.)

spec.title: Standard clearance between armature plate and magnet clutch pulley “a”:
